description: Generic wrapper for HTTP-driven external job runners — internal job APIs, less-common SaaS tools, GitHub Actions / Jenkins / CircleCI / Argo / Kestra style runners, or for prototyping before investing in a custom SDK. Users configure trigger / status / log endpoints in YAML; the component runs the trigger → poll → finalize loop, surfaces structured metadata, and honors all standard Dagster per-asset attributes. NOTE: prefer dedicated registry components (fivetran_assets, airbyte_assets, dbt_run_job, matillion_run_asset, etc.) when one exists for your vendor.

outputs.description: One MaterializeResult per asset, with external_run_id, duration, poll_count, plus user-extracted metadata.

base_url.label: Base URL
base_url.description: Base URL prepended to every endpoint path. Supports Jinja templating (env, secrets).

auth_resource_key.label: Auth Resource Key
auth_resource_key.description: Resource key of an HttpAuthResource subclass (BearerTokenAuth, BasicAuth, HeaderAuth, or a custom subclass). Resource must be wired in your Definitions resources dict.

assets.label: Assets
assets.description: One or more asset specs. Each emits a Dagster asset with its own trigger / status / logs configuration.

key.description: Dagster asset key. Use slashes for path-style keys (e.g. 'external/orders_sync').

description.description: Asset description shown in the Dagster UI.

kinds.description: List of asset kinds (compute kind tags). Defaults to ['http'].

metadata.description: Static metadata to attach to every materialization.

deps.description: Upstream Dagster asset keys for lineage.

automation_condition.description: AutomationCondition factory call as a Python expression. Supported helpers: on_cron('expr'), eager(), any_downstream_conditions().

retry_policy.description: Dagster RetryPolicy spec. Keys: max_retries, delay (seconds), backoff (linear|exponential), jitter (full|plus_minus).

code_version.description: Asset code version string (used for staleness tracking).

partition_type.description: daily | weekly | monthly | hourly | static | dynamic | multi.

partition_start.description: ISO date for time partitions (required for daily/weekly/monthly/hourly/multi).

partition_values.description: Comma-separated values for static partitions or the static dimension of multi.

dynamic_partition_name.description: Name of the DynamicPartitionsDefinition (required for partition_type='dynamic').

partition_dimensions.description: Multi-axis partitions: list of {name, type, ...} dicts. Mutually exclusive with partition_type.

trigger.description: HTTP request that starts the external run. Required.

path.description: Path appended to base_url. Supports Jinja templating.

path_params.description: Map substituted into {placeholder} segments of path.

body.description: Request body. String → sent verbatim (or as JSON if it parses); dict/list → JSON-serialized; null → no body. Supports {from_python: 'mod:fn'} escape hatch.

run_id.description: Extractor that pulls the external run-id out of the trigger response (jsonpath / regex / header). Required.

path.description: Path; {run_id} is auto-substituted from the trigger response.

timeout_seconds.description: Max wall-clock seconds to wait for a terminal status.

is_terminal.description: Condition that evaluates true when the external run has reached a terminal state (success or failure). Required.

is_success.description: Condition that, given a terminal response, evaluates true when the run succeeded. Optional — a fallback heuristic logs a warning.

metadata.description: Map of {metadata_key: extractor}. Extractors pull values from the terminal status response and attach them to the materialization.

next_cursor.description: Optional extractor for pagination cursor (from response body or headers).

patterns.description: List of {name, regex, group?, cast?, mode}. mode: first_match | count | exists.

failure_patterns.description: Regex list — if any match, override is_success → False even if the status said success.

name: HTTP External Asset
